title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: members, contributors, leaders pledge make participation community harassment-free experience everyone, regardless age, body size, visible invisible disability, ethnicity, sex characteristics, gender identity expression, level experience, education, socio-economic status, nationality, personal appearance, race, caste, color, religion, sexual identity orientation. pledge act interact ways contribute open, welcoming, diverse, inclusive, healthy community.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Examples behavior contributes positive environment community include: Demonstrating empathy kindness toward people respectful differing opinions, viewpoints, experiences Giving gracefully accepting constructive feedback Accepting responsibility apologizing affected mistakes, learning experience Focusing best just us individuals, overall community Examples unacceptable behavior include: use sexualized language imagery, sexual attention advances kind Trolling, insulting derogatory comments, personal political attacks Public private harassment Publishing others’ private information, physical email address, without explicit permission conduct reasonably considered inappropriate professional setting

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Community leaders responsible clarifying enforcing standards acceptable behavior take appropriate fair corrective action response behavior deem inappropriate, threatening, offensive, harmful. Community leaders right responsibility remove, edit, reject comments, commits, code, wiki edits, issues, contributions aligned Code Conduct, communicate reasons moderation decisions appropriate.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Code Conduct applies within community spaces, also applies individual officially representing community public spaces. Examples representing community include using official e-mail address, posting via official social media account, acting appointed representative online offline event.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Instances abusive, harassing, otherwise unacceptable behavior may reported community leaders responsible enforcement psychelzh@outlook.com. complaints reviewed investigated promptly fairly. community leaders obligated respect privacy security reporter incident.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Community leaders follow Community Impact Guidelines determining consequences action deem violation Code Conduct:

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Community Impact: Use inappropriate language behavior deemed unprofessional unwelcome community. Consequence: private, written warning community leaders, providing clarity around nature violation explanation behavior inappropriate. public apology may requested.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Community Impact: violation single incident series actions. Consequence: warning consequences continued behavior. interaction people involved, including unsolicited interaction enforcing Code Conduct, specified period time. includes avoiding interactions community spaces well external channels like social media. Violating terms may lead temporary permanent ban.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Community Impact: serious violation community standards, including sustained inappropriate behavior. Consequence: temporary ban sort interaction public communication community specified period time. public private interaction people involved, including unsolicited interaction enforcing Code Conduct, allowed period. Violating terms may lead permanent ban.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Community Impact: Demonstrating pattern violation community standards, including sustained inappropriate behavior, harassment individual, aggression toward disparagement classes individuals. Consequence: permanent ban sort public interaction within community.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct

title: Leakage-Safe Covariate Handling in CPM
text: covariates regressed full dataset cross-validation, held-test information leaks training pipeline. can inflate distort --sample performance. cpmr, leakage-safe behavior means: fit covariate regression fold’s training split ; apply learned regression parameters train test data inside fold; keep parameter-learning steps (example scaling model selection) inside CV well.

title: Leakage-Safe Covariate Handling in CPM
code: set.seed(123) n <- 80 p <- 120  # Synthetic connectivity matrix (rows = subjects, cols = edges) conmat <- matrix(rnorm(n * p), nrow = n, ncol = p)  # A synthetic covariate (for example age-like nuisance variable) covariates <- matrix(rnorm(n), ncol = 1)  # Build behavior with both signal and covariate effects edge_signal <- rowMeans(conmat[, 1:10, drop = FALSE]) behav <- 0.7 * edge_signal + 0.6 * covariates[, 1] + rnorm(n, sd = 0.5)  # Leakage-safe CV call: covariates are handled fold-wise inside resampling fit <- fit_resamples(   cpm_spec(),   conmat = conmat,   behav = behav,   covariates = covariates,   kfolds = 5 )  summary(fit) #> CPM resample summary: #>   Number of folds: 5 #>   Prediction error: #>     RMSE: #>       Combined: 0.514 #>       Positive: 0.510 #>       Negative: 0.529 #>     MAE: #>       Combined: 0.405 #>       Positive: 0.408 #>       Negative: 0.387 #>   Pooled correlations (Pearson): #>     Combined: 0.259 #>     Positive: 0.279 #>     Negative: -0.104 #>   Fold-wise correlations (Pearson): #>     Combined: 0.277 (SE 0.132) #>     Positive: 0.303 (SE 0.129) #>     Negative: -0.270

title: Leakage-Safe Covariate Handling in CPM
text: Using reusable cpm_spec() object helpful want CPM settings drive single-fit resampling analyses.
code: spec <- cpm_spec()  single_fit <- fit(   spec,   conmat = conmat,   behav = behav,   covariates = covariates )  resamples_fit <- fit_resamples(   spec,   conmat = conmat,   behav = behav,   covariates = covariates,   kfolds = 5,   return_edges = "sum" )  summary(resamples_fit) #> CPM resample summary: #>   Number of folds: 5 #>   Prediction error: #>     RMSE: #>       Combined: 0.514 #>       Positive: 0.511 #>       Negative: 0.539 #>     MAE: #>       Combined: 0.404 #>       Positive: 0.406 #>       Negative: 0.393 #>   Pooled correlations (Pearson): #>     Combined: 0.254 #>     Positive: 0.272 #>     Negative: -0.080 #>   Fold-wise correlations (Pearson): #>     Combined: 0.214 (SE 0.120) #>     Positive: 0.235 (SE 0.120) #>     Negative: -0.115 (SE 0.080) #>   Selected edges: #>     Positive: 1.50% #>     Negative: 0.33% head(resamples_fit$predictions) #>   row fold       real        both         pos          neg #> 1   1    4 -0.5862850 -0.13713172 -0.13713172 8.673617e-18 #> 2   2    4  0.7262082 -0.06258281 -0.06258281 8.673617e-18 #> 3   3    2  0.4776754  0.11181262  0.08055321 6.622293e-02 #> 4   4    3  0.4263198  0.14677926  0.14677926 0.000000e+00 #> 5   5    5 -1.5329525 -0.07744804 -0.07744804 1.908196e-17 #> 6   6    3  0.4996146  0.56813333  0.56813333 0.000000e+00 dim(resamples_fit$edges) #> [1] 120   2

title: Leakage-Safe Covariate Handling in CPM
text: large feature spaces, fold-wise edge storage can grow quickly. Use return_edges = "sum" keep fold-aggregated edge counts. Use return_edges = "none" need predictive performance.

title: Leakage-Safe Covariate Handling in CPM
text: Use checklist building CPM workflows: Split train/test folds parameter-learning step. Fit covariate regression training split . Apply training-fitted covariate regression fold’s test split. Keep scaling feature selection inside fold. comparing multiple settings, use nested CV model selection. Report CV design covariate strategy explicitly.

title: Leakage-Safe Covariate Handling in CPM
text: Snoek L, Miletic S, Scholte HS (2019). control confounds decoding analyses neuroimaging data. NeuroImage. https://doi.org/10.1016/j.neuroimage.2018.09.074 Scheinost D, Noble S, Horien C, et al. (2019). Ten simple rules predictive modeling individual differences neuroimaging. NeuroImage. https://doi.org/10.1016/j.neuroimage.2019.02.057 Rosenblatt M, Tejavibulya L, Jiang R, Noble S, Scheinost D (2024). Data leakage inflates prediction performance connectome-based machine learning models. Nature Communications. https://doi.org/10.1038/s41467-024-46150-w

title: Native Getting Started with cpmr
text: cpmr’s primary workflow native, matrix-first API built around cpm_spec() together fit() fit_resamples(). vignette walks : shape CPM inputs; fit single native CPM model; run leakage-safe resampling; inspect predictions selected edges; custom resamples missing-data handling work.

title: Native Getting Started with cpmr
text: CPM expects one row per subject one column per edge. conmat: n x p connectivity matrix; behav: length-n numeric outcome vector; covariates: optional n x q nuisance matrix.
code: library(cpmr)  withr::local_seed(123) n <- 80 p <- 200  conmat <- matrix(rnorm(n * p), nrow = n, ncol = p) covariates <- matrix(rnorm(n), ncol = 1) behav <- rowMeans(conmat[, 1:12, drop = FALSE]) +   0.5 * covariates[, 1] +   rnorm(n, sd = 0.5)

title: Native Getting Started with cpmr
text: Use fit() cpm_spec() object want native single fit explicit CPM parameters. single fit, fit() stores p x 2 edge-selection mask pos neg columns default. useful inspecting selected network, performance reported summary() still -sample.
code: fit_obj <- fit(   cpm_spec(thresh_method = "alpha", thresh_level = 0.05),   conmat = conmat,   behav = behav,   covariates = covariates )  fit_obj #> CPM fit: #>   Call: fit(object = cpm_spec(thresh_method = "alpha", thresh_level = 0.05),  #>     conmat = conmat, behav = behav, covariates = covariates) #>   Number of observations: 80 #>     Complete cases: 80 #>   Candidate edges: 200 #>   Parameters: #>     Covariates:       included #>     Threshold method: alpha #>     Threshold level:  0.05 #>     Bias correction:  yes summary(fit_obj) #> CPM summary: #>   Performance (Pearson): #>     Combined: 0.706 #>     Positive: 0.620 #>     Negative: 0.458 #>   Selected edges: #>     Positive: 4.00% #>     Negative: 2.50% dim(fit_obj$edges) #> [1] 200   2

title: Native Getting Started with cpmr
text: Use fit_resamples() cpm_spec() object --sample estimate predictive performance. keeps CPM-specific steps covariate handling, edge selection, model training inside resample fold.
code: resample_obj <- fit_resamples(   cpm_spec(),   conmat = conmat,   behav = behav,   covariates = covariates,   kfolds = 5 )  resample_obj #> CPM resamples: #>   Call: fit_resamples(object = cpm_spec(), conmat = conmat, behav = behav,  #>     covariates = covariates, kfolds = 5) #>   Number of folds: 5 #>   Number of observations: 80 #>     Complete cases: 80 #>   Edge storage: not stored #>   Use summary() for aggregate metrics. summary(resample_obj) #> CPM resample summary: #>   Number of folds: 5 #>   Prediction error: #>     RMSE: #>       Combined: 0.708 #>       Positive: 0.645 #>       Negative: 0.704 #>     MAE: #>       Combined: 0.574 #>       Positive: 0.516 #>       Negative: 0.559 #>   Pooled correlations (Pearson): #>     Combined: -0.093 #>     Positive: 0.059 #>     Negative: -0.185 #>   Fold-wise correlations (Pearson): #>     Combined: -0.075 (SE 0.046) #>     Positive: 0.029 (SE 0.054) #>     Negative: -0.181 (SE 0.099)

title: Native Getting Started with cpmr
text: Native resampling results always keep raw observation-level predictions object, summary() gives aggregate --fold report error metrics default summary correlations supplementary output. three outputs slightly different jobs: summary(resample_obj) prints default aggregate report; summary(resample_obj)[["metrics"]] exposes compact summary-level metric table stored summary object; resample_metrics(resample_obj) returns raw pooled fold-wise metric tables filtering, plotting, export. default, fit_resamples() uses return_edges = "none" skips edge storage. keeps resampling object light need predictive performance. also want fold-aggregated edge selection rates, request explicitly: predictions returns one row per original observation. na_action = "exclude" removed subjects fitting, rows still present fold value NA. return_edges = "sum", cpmr stores fold-summed edge counts edge. memory matters, keep default return_edges = "none" use return_edges = "" fold-wise edge arrays truly needed.
code: predictions <- resample_obj$predictions  resample_summary <- summary(resample_obj)  resample_summary #> CPM resample summary: #>   Number of folds: 5 #>   Prediction error: #>     RMSE: #>       Combined: 0.708 #>       Positive: 0.645 #>       Negative: 0.704 #>     MAE: #>       Combined: 0.574 #>       Positive: 0.516 #>       Negative: 0.559 #>   Pooled correlations (Pearson): #>     Combined: -0.093 #>     Positive: 0.059 #>     Negative: -0.185 #>   Fold-wise correlations (Pearson): #>     Combined: -0.075 (SE 0.046) #>     Positive: 0.029 (SE 0.054) #>     Negative: -0.181 (SE 0.099) head(resample_summary[["metrics"]]) #>    level metric prediction  estimate std_error method #> 1 pooled   rmse       both 0.7081353        NA   <NA> #> 2 pooled   rmse        pos 0.6448140        NA   <NA> #> 3 pooled   rmse        neg 0.7036247        NA   <NA> #> 4 pooled    mae       both 0.5743804        NA   <NA> #> 5 pooled    mae        pos 0.5162523        NA   <NA> #> 6 pooled    mae        neg 0.5592357        NA   <NA> head(predictions) #>   row fold       real         both          pos           neg #> 1   1    5 -0.6339456  0.342534590  0.342534590  8.673617e-18 #> 2   2    1  0.4116546 -0.478738601  0.028245399 -5.179443e-01 #> 3   3    1  0.2898583 -0.032769603  0.073529511 -1.070810e-01 #> 4   4    3  0.7870086 -0.001288122 -0.001288122 -3.469447e-18 #> 5   5    5  0.1169538 -0.023682986 -0.023682986  8.673617e-18 #> 6   6    3  0.7875511  0.255961736  0.255961736 -3.469447e-18 head(resample_metrics(resample_obj)) #>   fold n_assess metric prediction  estimate #> 1    1       16   rmse       both 0.6175234 #> 2    1       16   rmse        pos 0.5564055 #> 3    1       16   rmse        neg 0.5860971 #> 4    2       16   rmse       both 0.8263091 #> 5    2       16   rmse        pos 0.6408348 #> 6    2       16   rmse        neg 0.8491404 edge_resample_obj <- fit_resamples(   cpm_spec(),   conmat = conmat,   behav = behav,   covariates = covariates,   kfolds = 5,   return_edges = "sum" )  dim(edge_resample_obj$edges) #> [1] 200   2

title: Native Getting Started with cpmr
text: already partition scheme, pass resamples. Custom resamples must: supplied assessment-row indices; cover every complete-case subject exactly ; overlap across folds. na_action = "exclude", indices still refer original row numbers complete-case subjects kept analysis.
code: custom_resamples <- split(   seq_len(n),   cut(seq_len(n), breaks = 4, labels = FALSE) )  custom_obj <- fit_resamples(   cpm_spec(),   conmat = conmat,   behav = behav,   resamples = custom_resamples,   return_edges = "none" )  summary(custom_obj) #> CPM resample summary: #>   Number of folds: 4 #>   Prediction error: #>     RMSE: #>       Combined: 0.936 #>       Positive: 0.905 #>       Negative: 0.944 #>     MAE: #>       Combined: 0.679 #>       Positive: 0.642 #>       Negative: 0.736 #>   Pooled correlations (Pearson): #>     Combined: 0.142 #>     Positive: 0.173 #>     Negative: -0.008 #>   Fold-wise correlations (Pearson): #>     Combined: 0.158 (SE 0.121) #>     Positive: 0.247 (SE 0.129) #>     Negative: 0.026 (SE 0.113)

title: Native Getting Started with cpmr
text: Set na_action = "exclude" want cpmr fit complete cases preserving original row positions outputs. behavior matters need merge predictions back subject-level metadata resampling.
code: behav_with_na <- behav behav_with_na[c(3, 11)] <- NA_real_  na_obj <- fit_resamples(   cpm_spec(),   conmat = conmat,   behav = behav_with_na,   kfolds = 5,   na_action = "exclude" )  na_predictions <- na_obj$predictions na_predictions[na_predictions$row %in% c(3, 11), ] #>    row fold real both pos neg #> 3    3   NA   NA   NA  NA  NA #> 11  11   NA   NA   NA  NA  NA

title: Native Getting Started with cpmr
text: comfortable native path: read workflow-selection article guidance use native cpm_spec() workflow different settings; read leakage-focused article need detailed covariate handling example; keep cpm_spec() object around want explicit reusable parameter object.

title: Choosing a Workflow in cpmr
text: cpmr organized around one native workflow: create cpm_spec() object, use fit() fit_resamples() depending question want answer. main choice different API families. : fit() single -sample CPM fit; fit_resamples() --sample resampling; fit_resamples(..., resamples = ...) already partition scheme want enforce.

title: Choosing a Workflow in cpmr
text: analyses, start cpm_spec() reuse across single-fit resampling calls. workflow best fit care : large edge spaces; repeated resampling; leakage-safe covariate handling; future CPM-specific performance improvements fold-level reuse.
code: library(cpmr)  set.seed(123) conmat <- matrix(rnorm(80 * 200), nrow = 80) behav <- rnorm(80) spec <- cpm_spec()  fit_obj <- fit(spec, conmat = conmat, behav = behav) resample_obj <- fit_resamples(spec, conmat = conmat, behav = behav, kfolds = 5)  summary(fit_obj) #> CPM summary: #>   Performance (Pearson): #>     Combined: 0.508 #>     Positive: 0.300 #>     Negative: 0.430 #>   Selected edges: #>     Positive: 0.50% #>     Negative: 1.00% summary(resample_obj) #> CPM resample summary: #>   Number of folds: 5 #>   Prediction error: #>     RMSE: #>       Combined: 1.120 #>       Positive: 1.149 #>       Negative: 1.137 #>     MAE: #>       Combined: 0.873 #>       Positive: 0.900 #>       Negative: 0.914 #>   Pooled correlations (Pearson): #>     Combined: -0.055 #>     Positive: -0.141 #>     Negative: -0.031 #>   Fold-wise correlations (Pearson): #>     Combined: -0.041 (SE 0.202) #>     Positive: -0.161 (SE 0.160) #>     Negative: 0.025 (SE 0.194) head(summary(resample_obj)[["metrics"]]) #>    level metric prediction  estimate std_error method #> 1 pooled   rmse       both 1.1203509        NA   <NA> #> 2 pooled   rmse        pos 1.1492880        NA   <NA> #> 3 pooled   rmse        neg 1.1373148        NA   <NA> #> 4 pooled    mae       both 0.8728802        NA   <NA> #> 5 pooled    mae        pos 0.9000038        NA   <NA> #> 6 pooled    mae        neg 0.9139934        NA   <NA>

title: Choosing a Workflow in cpmr
text: Use fit() want inspect one fitted CPM model current data. Use fit_resamples() want --sample performance estimate custom resampling design. rule thumb: use summary(fit_obj) tidy(fit_obj) single-fit summaries; use summary(resample_obj) default aggregate resampling report; use summary(resample_obj)[["metrics"]] resample_metrics(resample_obj) need structured metric tables instead printed output.

title: Authors and Citation
text: Liang Zhang. Author, maintainer.

code: @Manual{,   title = {cpmr: Connectome Predictive Modelling in R},   author = {Liang Zhang},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 0.1.1.9000},   url = {https://github.com/psychelzh/cpmr}, }

title: Connectome Predictive Modelling in R
text: cpmr package implements connectome predictive modeling (CPM) R. primary workflow native matrix-first: pass subject--edge matrix together behavioral vector, cpmr handles CPM-specific training, resampling, leakage-safe preprocessing inside package code. package relies Rfast row-oriented calculation.

title: Connectome Predictive Modelling in R
text: can install released version cpmr CRAN : can install development version cpmr r-universe :
code: install.packages("cpmr") install.packages("cpmr", repos = c("https://psychelzh.r-universe.dev", getOption("repos")))

title: Connectome Predictive Modelling in R
text: Shape connectivity matrix subjects--edges matrix, row contains edge vector one subject, pair behavioral vector. native workflow uses cpm_spec() together fit(). Cross-validated resampling uses fit_resamples() specification object: summary(resample_obj) gives default aggregate report, pooled --fold error metrics shown first correlations reported supplementary statistics. Use resample_metrics(resample_obj) want pooled fold-wise metric tables directly.
code: library(cpmr)  withr::local_seed(123) conmat <- matrix(rnorm(100 * 1000), nrow = 100) behav <- rnorm(100) fit_obj <- fit(cpm_spec(), conmat = conmat, behav = behav)  fit_obj #> CPM fit: #>   Call: fit(object = cpm_spec(), conmat = conmat, behav = behav) #>   Number of observations: 100 #>     Complete cases: 100 #>   Candidate edges: 1000 #>   Parameters: #>     Covariates:       none #>     Threshold method: alpha #>     Threshold level:  0.01 #>     Bias correction:  yes summary(fit_obj) #> CPM summary: #>   Performance (Pearson): #>     Combined: 0.676 #>     Positive: 0.595 #>     Negative: 0.387 #>   Selected edges: #>     Positive: 0.70% #>     Negative: 0.20% resample_obj <- fit_resamples(cpm_spec(), conmat = conmat, behav = behav, kfolds = 5)  summary(resample_obj) #> CPM resample summary: #>   Number of folds: 5 #>   Prediction error: #>     RMSE: #>       Combined: 1.243 #>       Positive: 1.205 #>       Negative: 1.200 #>     MAE: #>       Combined: 0.947 #>       Positive: 0.962 #>       Negative: 0.905 #>   Pooled correlations (Pearson): #>     Combined: -0.104 #>     Positive: -0.072 #>     Negative: -0.074 #>   Fold-wise correlations (Pearson): #>     Combined: -0.057 (SE 0.062) #>     Positive: 0.008 (SE 0.089) #>     Negative: -0.036 (SE 0.077) head(resample_obj$predictions) #>   row fold        real        both        pos         neg #> 1   1    1  0.26499342 0.492748241 -0.2058990  0.76974455 #> 2   2    5  1.83074748 0.323127546  0.2918329  0.05747766 #> 3   3    5 -0.05937826 0.901452079  1.1152311 -0.34018567 #> 4   4    3 -0.05320937 0.659647280  0.5558721  0.26308893 #> 5   5    1  0.43790418 0.002067539  0.2977574 -0.37106308 #> 6   6    4  1.33744904 0.659172053  0.5130919  0.37428531 dim(resample_obj$edges) #> NULL head(resample_metrics(resample_obj)) #>   fold n_assess metric prediction  estimate #> 1    1       20   rmse       both 1.1115539 #> 2    1       20   rmse        pos 1.1024986 #> 3    1       20   rmse        neg 1.0753358 #> 4    2       20   rmse       both 0.9556034 #> 5    2       20   rmse        pos 0.9872521 #> 6    2       20   rmse        neg 1.0424502

title: Connectome Predictive Modelling in R
text: cpmr treats native workflow primary package path: use fit(cpm_spec(), ...) fit_resamples(cpm_spec(), ...) native CPM analyses; keep cpm_spec() object around want explicit, reusable parameter object. matters: CPM often needs leakage-safe fold-local preprocessing can benefit future fold-level caching threshold-aware optimization. workloads fit native cpmr runners better generic orchestration layer.

title: Connectome Predictive Modelling in R
text: Please note cpmr project released Contributor Code Conduct. contributing project, agree abide terms.

title: cpm Fitted Object — cpm
text: cpm object returned fit() fitting cpm_spec() single dataset.

title: cpm Fitted Object — cpm
text: cpm object list following elements: call Matched call used fitting. spec originating cpm_spec object. params Parameter list used fit time. predictions Data frame observation-level outputs columns row, real, , pos, neg. edges Stored single-fit edge mask p x 2 logical matrix pos neg columns. model Trained CPM model components used prediction.

title: cpm_resamples Resampling Object — cpm_resamples
text: cpm_resamples object returned fit_resamples() stores observation-level predictions together resampling structure produced .

title: cpm_resamples Resampling Object — cpm_resamples
text: cpm_resamples object list following elements: call Matched call used resampling. spec originating cpm_spec object. params Parameter list used resampling run. predictions Data frame observation-level predictions fold IDs. edges Stored edge output based return_edges (NULL/matrix/array). folds List assessment-row indices fold.

what: Define a CPM model specification — cpm_spec
title: Define a CPM model specification — cpm_spec
text: Create lightweight specification object stores modeling parameters required fit connectome-based predictive model later fit() fit_resamples().

title: Define a CPM model specification — cpm_spec
code: cpm_spec(   thresh_method = c("alpha", "sparsity"),   thresh_level = 0.01,   bias_correct = TRUE )  # S3 method for class 'cpm_spec' fit(   object,   conmat,   behav,   ...,   covariates = NULL,   na_action = c("fail", "exclude") )  # S3 method for class 'cpm_spec' fit_resamples(   object,   conmat,   behav,   ...,   covariates = NULL,   resamples = NULL,   kfolds = NULL,   return_edges = c("none", "sum", "all"),   na_action = c("fail", "exclude") )

title: Define a CPM model specification — cpm_spec
text: thresh_method, thresh_level threshold method level used edge selection. "alpha", edges selected thresholding absolute correlation critical value implied thresh_level. "sparsity", thresh_level treated proportion edges selected lower upper tails correlation distribution. bias_correct Logical value indicating connectome data bias-corrected. TRUE, connectome data centered scaled unit variance based training data model fitting prediction. See Rapuano et al. (2020) details. object cpm_spec object. conmat matrix connectome data. Observations row, edges column. behav numeric outcome vector one value per observation conmat. Row column matrices accepted converted drop(). ... future extension. Currently ignored. covariates matrix covariates. Observations row, variables column. NULL, covariates used. Vectors converted single-column matrices. na_action character string indicating action missing values found inputs. "fail" stops immediately required value missing. "exclude" fits complete cases keeps original row layout returned predictions. resamples Optional list assessment indices defining resamples. element must integer vector indexing rows conmat. NULL, folds generated kfolds. kfolds Number folds used resamples NULL. NULL, set number complete-case observations (LOOCV). return_edges character string indicating return value selected edges. "none" skips edge storage, "sum" stores fold counts selected edge, "" stores fold-wise edge masks.

title: Define a CPM model specification — cpm_spec
text: cpm_spec() returns cpm_spec object can reused across calls fit() fit_resamples(). fit() returns cpm object single -sample fit. Single-fit CPM objects always store selected edge mask. fit_resamples() returns cpm_resamples object containing observation-level predictions, resampling folds, optional stored edges. Call summary.cpm_resamples() default aggregate report, resample_metrics() want pooled fold-wise metrics tabular form.

title: Define a CPM model specification — cpm_spec
code: spec <- cpm_spec(thresh_level = 0.01) spec #> CPM specification: #>   Threshold method: alpha #>   Threshold level:  0.01 #>   Bias correction:  yes  conmat <- matrix(rnorm(100 * 100), nrow = 100) behav <- rnorm(100) fit_obj <- fit(spec, conmat = conmat, behav = behav) summary(fit_obj) #> CPM summary: #>   Performance (Pearson): #>     Combined: 0.258 #>     Positive: 0.258 #>     Negative: NA #>   Selected edges: #>     Positive: 1.00% #>     Negative: 0.00%  resample_obj <- fit_resamples(spec, conmat = conmat, behav = behav, kfolds = 5) summary(resample_obj) #> CPM resample summary: #>   Number of folds: 5 #>   Prediction error: #>     RMSE: #>       Combined: 1.078 #>       Positive: 1.078 #>       Negative: 1.126 #>     MAE: #>       Combined: 0.818 #>       Positive: 0.818 #>       Negative: 0.836 #>   Pooled correlations (Pearson): #>     Combined: 0.036 #>     Positive: 0.036 #>     Negative: -0.205 #>   Fold-wise correlations (Pearson): #>     Combined: 0.095 #>     Positive: 0.095 #>     Negative: -0.456

what: cpmr: Connectome Predictive Modelling in R — cpmr-package
title: cpmr: Connectome Predictive Modelling in R — cpmr-package
text: Connectome Predictive Modelling (CPM) (Shen et al. (2017) doi:10.1038/nprot.2016.178 ) method predict individual differences behaviour brain functional connectivity. 'cpmr' provides simple yet efficient implementation method.

what: Extract resampling metrics from a cpm_resamples object — resample_metrics
title: Extract resampling metrics from a cpm_resamples object — resample_metrics
text: Extract resampling metrics cpm_resamples object

title: Extract resampling metrics from a cpm_resamples object — resample_metrics
code: resample_metrics(   x,   level = c("foldwise", "pooled"),   metrics = c("rmse", "mae", "correlation"),   correlation_method = c("pearson", "spearman") )

title: Extract resampling metrics from a cpm_resamples object — resample_metrics
text: x cpm_resamples object. level level metric output return. Use "foldwise" one row per fold, metric, prediction stream, "pooled" one row per metric prediction stream computed across --fold predictions. metrics metrics include. Supported values "rmse", "mae", "correlation". correlation_method Correlation method used metrics includes "correlation".

title: Extract resampling metrics from a cpm_resamples object — resample_metrics
text: data frame. level = "foldwise", returned columns fold, n_assess, metric, prediction, estimate. level = "pooled", returned columns metric, prediction, estimate.

title: Extract resampling metrics from a cpm_resamples object — resample_metrics
text: Use resample_metrics() want resampling metrics tabular form downstream inspection plotting. Compared summary.cpm_resamples(), helper less opinionated: can return pooled metrics across --fold predictions raw fold-wise metrics used build aggregate summaries.

title: Extract resampling metrics from a cpm_resamples object — resample_metrics
code: withr::local_seed(123) conmat <- matrix(rnorm(200), nrow = 20) behav <- rowMeans(conmat[, 1:5, drop = FALSE]) + rnorm(20, sd = 0.2) res <- fit_resamples(cpm_spec(), conmat = conmat, behav = behav, kfolds = 4)  head(resample_metrics(res)) #>   fold n_assess metric prediction  estimate #> 1    1        5   rmse       both 0.4106618 #> 2    1        5   rmse        pos 0.4106618 #> 3    1        5   rmse        neg 0.4106618 #> 4    2        5   rmse       both 0.5779391 #> 5    2        5   rmse        pos 0.5779391 #> 6    2        5   rmse        neg 0.6351870 resample_metrics(res, level = "pooled", metrics = "correlation") #>        metric prediction   estimate #> 1 correlation       both  0.2428599 #> 2 correlation        pos  0.2428599 #> 3 correlation        neg -0.1949248

title: Summary of a cpm object. — summary.cpm
text: object object class cpm. ... parameters passed function. method character vector indicating method used calculate correlation real predicted values. x object class cpm_summary.

title: Summary of a cpm object. — summary.cpm
text: list class cpm_summary containing: metrics data frame columns level, metric, prediction, estimate, std_error, method. Single-fit CPM summaries store correlation metrics level = "single". edges logical matrix indicating edges selected fitted CPM model. params list parameters used summary.

title: Summarize a cpm_resamples object — summary.cpm_resamples
code: # S3 method for class 'cpm_resamples' summary(object, ...)  # S3 method for class 'cpm_resamples_summary' print(x, ...)

title: Summarize a cpm_resamples object — summary.cpm_resamples
text: object object class cpm_resamples. ... future extension. Currently ignored. x object class cpm_resamples_summary.

title: Summarize a cpm_resamples object — summary.cpm_resamples
text: cpm_resamples_summary object following elements: metrics data frame columns level, metric, prediction, estimate, std_error, method. Resample summaries store pooled errors pooled correlations level = "pooled", fold-wise correlation summaries level = "foldwise". edges Aggregated edge-selection rates, NULL edges stored. params list containing summary-relevant resampling settings.

title: Summarize a cpm_resamples object — summary.cpm_resamples
text: summary.cpm_resamples() designed give compact default report. leads pooled --fold error metrics (RMSE MAE), reports pooled fold-wise correlations supplementary statistics. keeps default summary usable even fold-wise correlations undefined resampling schemes, leave-one-resampling.

title: Summarize a cpm_resamples object — summary.cpm_resamples
code: withr::local_seed(123) conmat <- matrix(rnorm(200), nrow = 20) behav <- rowMeans(conmat[, 1:5, drop = FALSE]) + rnorm(20, sd = 0.2) res <- fit_resamples(cpm_spec(), conmat = conmat, behav = behav, kfolds = 4)  summary(res) #> CPM resample summary: #>   Number of folds: 4 #>   Prediction error: #>     RMSE: #>       Combined: 0.432 #>       Positive: 0.432 #>       Negative: 0.452 #>     MAE: #>       Combined: 0.365 #>       Positive: 0.365 #>       Negative: 0.358 #>   Pooled correlations (Pearson): #>     Combined: 0.243 #>     Positive: 0.243 #>     Negative: -0.195 #>   Fold-wise correlations (Pearson): #>     Combined: 0.454 #>     Positive: 0.454 #>     Negative: NA

title: Tidy a cpm object — tidy.cpm
text: Tidy cpm object

title: Tidy a cpm object — tidy.cpm
code: # S3 method for class 'cpm' tidy(x, ..., component = c("performance", "edges"))

title: Tidy a cpm object — tidy.cpm
text: x cpm object. ... Additional arguments passed summary(). component character vector indicating component tidy.

title: Tidy a cpm object — tidy.cpm
text: tibble columns storing parameters cpm object columns depending component argument: component = "performance": method method used calculate correlation real predicted values. pos correlation real predicted values positive edges. neg correlation real predicted values negative edges. component = "edges": pos logical vector indicating whether edge selected fitted CPM model (positive). neg logical vector indicating whether edge selected fitted CPM model (negative).

title: cpmr (development version)
text: Removed legacy cpm() entry point deprecated confounds alias. Use fit(cpm_spec(...), ...) / fit_resamples(cpm_spec(...), ...) native workflow. fit() now performs single fit; resampling handled fit_resamples(). cpm_resamples longer stores redundant resample metrics, collect_*() helpers removed. Use summary() aggregated resample results predictions, edges, folds raw outputs.

title: cpmr (development version)
text: Added cpm_spec() native interface fit() fit_resamples(), made single-fit resample result objects consistent. Added summary.cpm_resamples(), now reports pooled --fold error metrics default keeps pooled / fold-wise correlations supplementary statistics. Added resample_metrics() direct access pooled fold-wise metric tables cpm_resamples object. Added native-first documentation, including getting-started vignette reorganized pkgdown reference pages.

title: cpmr (development version)
text: Refactored internals expanded test coverage around fit, summary, edge, resampling behavior. Hardened resample validation fold must retain least 3 complete-case training observations.

title: cpmr 0.1.1
text: CRAN release: 2026-03-11

title: cpmr 0.1.1
text: Renamed internal constant networks includes corr_types inc_edges, respectively, better reflect purpose. addition, documentation mentioned networks updated use correlation types better reflect meaning. Reorganized R/cpm.R cpm() remains entry-point function top, followed print.cpm(), internal helpers grouped . Improved test architecture explicit internal-helper coverage complete-case fold invariants.

title: cpmr 0.1.1
text: Refactored internal cpm() workflow smaller helpers (normalize_inputs, resolve_include_cases, apply_confounds_regression, etc.) preserving user-facing behavior. Removed obsolete internal alias regress_counfounds(). Removed broad Rfast namespace import favor explicit Rfast:: calls. Updated summary.cpm() edge summarization path avoid mutating local object fields.

title: cpmr 0.1.0
text: CRAN release: 2024-10-06

title: cpmr 0.1.0
text: Added summary() method summarize results CPM analysis (#8). Added tidy() method tidy results CPM analysis (#10). Support na_action argument cpm() function handle missing values input data (#2).

title: cpmr 0.1.0
text: Added params cpm() output store input arguments (#14). Let "sum" default value return_edges argument. Let first two dimensions edges output edges networks, respectively. Polish print method cpm class.

title: cpmr 0.0.9
text: CRAN release: 2024-06-08

title: cpmr 0.0.9
text: Added support row/column matrix input behavior confounds data.

title: cpmr 0.0.9
text: Added data checks ensure input data correct format.

title: cpmr 0.0.8
text: CRAN release: 2024-03-14 Added return_edges argument optionally set return edges output.

title: cpmr 0.0.7
text: Convert back older version confounds treating.

title: cpmr 0.0.6
text: Ensure confounds regression now used feature selection.

title: cpmr 0.0.5
text: Fixed confounds treatment. Now confounds used feature selection model fitting.

title: cpmr 0.0.4
text: Ensure sparsity threshold method work expect. improvements code quality.

title: cpmr 0.0.3

title: cpmr 0.0.2
